brief_title: Tau Aggregation Inhibitor in Mild Cognitive Impairment and Mild Alzheimer's Disease
official_title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study of a Novel Tau Aggregation Inhibitor (TAI-201) in Patients with Mild Cognitive Impairment or Mild Alzheimer's Disease
brief_summary: This Phase 2 study evaluates the safety, tolerability, and preliminary efficacy of TAI-201, a novel tau aggregation inhibitor, in patients with mild cognitive impairment or mild Alzheimer's disease. Primary endpoints include safety and change in plasma p-tau217 from baseline to 24 weeks.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Adults aged 55-80 years

2. Clinical diagnosis of mild cognitive impairment due to Alzheimer's disease or mild Alzheimer's disease

3. MMSE score >= 18

4. Positive tau PET scan or elevated CSF p-tau

5. CDR Global Score of 0.5 or 1.0

6. On stable medications for at least 4 weeks prior to screening

7. Reliable informant available for study visits

8. Able to complete neuropsychological assessments in English or local language

9. Adequate hepatic function (ALT/AST <= 2x ULN)

10. Written informed consent

11. Willing to undergo lumbar puncture at baseline and week 24
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Moderate or severe Alzheimer's disease (MMSE < 18)

2. Significant cerebrovascular disease on MRI (modified Hachinski score > 4)

3. Other neurodegenerative diseases (Parkinson's disease, frontotemporal dementia, Lewy body dementia)

4. Current use of strong CYP2D6 inhibitors

5. History of significant head trauma with loss of consciousness > 30 minutes

6. Major depressive episode within past 6 months

7. Uncontrolled diabetes (HbA1c > 9%)

8. eGFR < 30 mL/min/1.73m²

9. Known hypersensitivity to study drug components

inclusion_criteria.text: Clinical diagnosis of mild cognitive impairment due to Alzheimer's disease or mild Alzheimer's disease
Positive tau PET scan or elevated CSF p-tau
On stable medications for at least 4 weeks prior to screening
Reliable informant available for study visits
Able to complete neuropsychological assessments in English or local language
Willing to undergo lumbar puncture at baseline and week 24

exclusion_criteria.text: Significant cerebrovascular disease on MRI (modified Hachinski score > 4)
Other neurodegenerative diseases (Parkinson's disease, frontotemporal dementia, Lewy body dementia)
Current use of strong CYP2D6 inhibitors
History of significant head trauma with loss of consciousness > 30 minutes
